basic_details.job_title: KSP Recruitment 2026 – Karnataka Police Constable Notification Out for 3991 Vacancies | Apply Online by 3rd July
basic_details.short_information: Karnataka State Police is recruiting 3991 Police Constable (Civil) posts across two cadres with PUC / equivalent qualification, last date 03 July 2026.

application_fee.details: The application fee for the KSP Constable recruitment must be paid online only, and is non-refundable under all circumstances. Candidates must ensure payment is completed before 4th July 2026.

age_limit.details: The minimum age for all candidates is 18 years. The upper age limit varies by category as follows: | Category Maximum Age General - Male, Female & 3rd Gender (Direct) 33 years SC / ST / Cat-1 / Cat-2A, 2B, 3A, 3B (Direct) 35 years Budakattu (Forest Area) SC Candidates 38 years Ex-Servicemen (General) 38 years Ex-Servicemen (SC/ST/OBC) 40 years | Age is calculated as of the notification date. Candidates should refer to the official PDF for precise age cut-off dates.

qualification.details: Before submitting your PC application for 2026 Karnataka, carefully verify that you meet all eligibility conditions. Candidates who do not meet the educational, age, or language criteria will be disqualified at any stage of the process. | Candidates must have passed Class 12 / PUC or an equivalent examination from a recognised board. This includes: | Class 12 passed from CBSE, ICSE, any State Board, or equivalent Class 12 equivalent from NIOS (National Institute of Open Schooling) A 2-year ITC/Diploma (NVBT) course with one language and one academic subject from NIOS A 3-year Diploma from a recognised Polytechnic institution (treated as equivalent to PUC for direct/compassionate recruitment) | Kannada Language Requirement: Candidates must have studied Kannada as a first or second language up to SSLC/10th standard. Those who have not done so must pass a Kannada Language Test conducted by KEA. | The minimum age for all candidates is 18 years. The upper age limit varies by category as follows: | Age is calculated as of the notification date. Candidates should refer to the official PDF for precise age cut-off dates.

salary_details.details: One of the most attractive aspects of the KSP Recruitment 2026 is the government pay structure. Karnataka police constables are placed at Pay Level 7 under the 7th Pay Commission, with a monthly pay scale of Rs. 37,500 - Rs. 76,100. In addition to the base salary, candidates are eligible for: | Dearness Allowance (DA) House Rent Allowance (HRA) Travel Allowance (TA) National Pension Scheme (NPS) benefits Other Karnataka Government allowances | Training and Probation: | Selected candidates undergo 9 months of basic police training at designated Police Training Schools in Karnataka. Candidates are on probation for 2 years and 6 months after formal appointment. Candidates who fail during training may be removed from service.

The Karnataka police constable selection process 2026 consists of four clearly defined stages. Candidates must qualify each stage to proceed to the next. There is no direct interview; merit is determined through the written exam and physical assessments.
The written test is the first and most critical stage of the pc recruitment 2026 Karnataka. It determines the candidates who will move on to physical testing.
Exam Timings:
Notification 1 (KK Cadre): 2:30 PM - 4:00 PM on 2nd August 2026 Notification 2 (General Cadre): 10:30 AM - 12:00 PM on 2nd August 2026
Subjects Covered:
General Knowledge & Current Affairs General Science Geography Indian History Indian Constitution Karnataka State Affairs Mental Ability & Reasoning Ethics
Candidates who qualify the written exam must appear for physical measurements. Below are the height and chest requirements:
Candidates who pass the PST will then undergo the physical endurance test. All events must be completed within the prescribed standards.
Shortlisted candidates (in a 1:2 ratio per vacancy) will undergo a comprehensive medical examination by a Medical Board. This includes vision testing
hearing assessment
and musculoskeletal evaluation to ensure candidates are physically fit for active police duty.
Candidates who clear the medical examination must present original academic certificates
domicile proof
category certificates
and other relevant documents for final verification.

pattern_table.Subject: General Knowledge, Science, Geography, History, Indian Constitution, State Affairs, Mental Ability, Ethics

exam_pattern.details: This section gives a concise view of the pc exam date 2026 Karnataka and the structure of the written test, helping candidates plan their preparation effectively. | Key Points to Remember: | Each correct answer carries 1 mark Each wrong answer results in a deduction of 0.25 marks Unanswered questions carry no penalty Minimum qualifying score is 30 marks

how_to_apply: The KSP Constable apply online 2026 process is conducted through the KEA (Karnataka Examinations Authority) portal. Follow these steps carefully to submit a valid application for Karnataka police constable recruitment 2026.
Step 1: Visit the official KEA recruitment portal at https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/
Step 2: Click on the active link for KSP Civil Police Constable Recruitment 2026 (Adv. No. 01 or 02, depending on which cadre you are eligible for).
Step 3: Register by entering your personal, educational, and category details accurately.
Step 4: Choose the cadre (Kalyana-Karnataka or General) you are applying for. Note: only one application per notification is permitted.
Step 5: Upload a recent passport-size photograph (JPEG, 50KB-200KB) and your signature (JPEG, 50KB-70KB).
Step 6: Pay the required application fee online through the portal.
Step 7: Download and save a copy of the submitted application form and the fee payment receipt.

content_sections.heading: Karnataka Police Constable Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: Karnataka State Police is recruiting 3991 Police Constable (Civil) posts across two cadres with PUC / equivalent qualification, last date 03 July 2026.

content_sections.heading: KSP Constable Notification 2026 - Important Dates
content_sections.text: Missing a deadline can disqualify your candidacy altogether. Below are all the critical dates in the KSP Constable application 2026 Karnataka, listed clearly so you can plan your application well ahead of time. Important: Apply well before the last date to avoid website congestion or technical difficulties. Do not wait until the deadline.

rows.event: Last Date to Pay Application Fee

content_sections.heading: KSP Constable Vacancy 2026 - Category-Wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: A total of 3,991 Civil Police Constable vacancies have been announced under two separate notifications. The vacancies are split between the Kalyana-Karnataka cadre (reserved under Article 371-J) and the General (Mikkilida/Okkalida) cadre. Candidates can apply for only one notification at a time and must choose the cadre applicable to them.

content_sections.heading: KSP Constable Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Before submitting your PC application for 2026 Karnataka, carefully verify that you meet all eligibility conditions. Candidates who do not meet the educational, age, or language criteria will be disqualified at any stage of the process. Candidates must have passed Class 12 / PUC or an equivalent examination from a recognised board. This includes: Class 12 passed from CBSE, ICSE, any State Board, or equivalent Class 12 equivalent from NIOS (National Institute of Open Schooling) A 2-year ITC/Diploma (NVBT) course with one language and one academic subject from NIOS A 3-year Diploma from a recognised Polytechnic institution (treated as equivalent to PUC for direct/compassionate recruitment) Kannada Language Requirement: Candidates must have studied Kannada as a first or second language up to SSLC/10th standard. Those who have not done so must pass a Kannada Language Test conducted by KEA. The minimum age for all candidates is 18 years. The upper age limit varies by category as follows: Age is calculated as of the notification date. Candidates should refer to the official PDF for precise age cut-off dates.

content_sections.heading: KSP Constable Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee for the KSP Constable recruitment must be paid online only, and is non-refundable under all circumstances. Candidates must ensure payment is completed before 4th July 2026.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for KSP Constable Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The KSP Constable apply online 2026 process is conducted through the KEA (Karnataka Examinations Authority) portal. Follow these steps carefully to submit a valid application for Karnataka police constable recruitment 2026. Step 1: Visit the official KEA recruitment portal at https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/ Step 2: Click on the active link for KSP Civil Police Constable Recruitment 2026 (Adv. No. 01 or 02, depending on which cadre you are eligible for). Step 3: Register by entering your personal, educational, and category details accurately. Step 4: Choose the cadre (Kalyana-Karnataka or General) you are applying for. Note: only one application per notification is permitted. Step 5: Upload a recent passport-size photograph (JPEG, 50KB-200KB) and your signature (JPEG, 50KB-70KB). Step 6: Pay the required application fee online through the portal. Step 7: Download and save a copy of the submitted application form and the fee payment receipt. Caution: No edits or corrections are permitted after final submission. Double-check all entries before clicking submit. Apply Link: https://ksp-recruitment.in/

content_sections.heading: KSP Constable Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: The Karnataka police constable selection process 2026 consists of four clearly defined stages. Candidates must qualify each stage to proceed to the next. There is no direct interview; merit is determined through the written exam and physical assessments. The written test is the first and most critical stage of the pc recruitment 2026 Karnataka. It determines the candidates who will move on to physical testing. Exam Timings: Notification 1 (KK Cadre): 2:30 PM - 4:00 PM on 2nd August 2026 Notification 2 (General Cadre): 10:30 AM - 12:00 PM on 2nd August 2026 Subjects Covered: General Knowledge & Current Affairs General Science Geography Indian History Indian Constitution Karnataka State Affairs Mental Ability & Reasoning Ethics Candidates who qualify the written exam must appear for physical measurements. Below are the height and chest requirements: Candidates who pass the PST will then undergo the physical endurance test. All events must be completed within the prescribed standards. Shortlisted candidates (in a 1:2 ratio per vacancy) will undergo a comprehensive medical examination by a Medical Board. This includes vision testing, hearing assessment, and musculoskeletal evaluation to ensure candidates are physically fit for active police duty. Candidates who clear the medical examination must present original academic certificates, domicile proof, category certificates, and other relevant documents for final verification.

content_sections.heading: KSP Constable Salary 2026
content_sections.text: One of the most attractive aspects of the KSP Recruitment 2026 is the government pay structure. Karnataka police constables are placed at Pay Level 7 under the 7th Pay Commission, with a monthly pay scale of Rs. 37,500 - Rs. 76,100. In addition to the base salary, candidates are eligible for: Dearness Allowance (DA) House Rent Allowance (HRA) Travel Allowance (TA) National Pension Scheme (NPS) benefits Other Karnataka Government allowances Training and Probation: Selected candidates undergo 9 months of basic police training at designated Police Training Schools in Karnataka. Candidates are on probation for 2 years and 6 months after formal appointment. Candidates who fail during training may be removed from service.

faq.question: Q1. What is KSP Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: KSP Recruitment 2026 refers to the Karnataka State Police's official recruitment drive to fill 3,991 Civil Police Constable vacancies across two cadres - the Kalyana-Karnataka cadre (596 posts) and the General/Mikkilida cadre (3,395 posts). Applications are accepted online through the KEA portal from 8th June to 3rd July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for KSP Constable Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online for KSP Constable Recruitment 2026 is 3rd July 2026. Candidates can pay the application fee until 4th July 2026. No applications will be accepted after these deadlines.

faq.question: Q3. What is the PC exam date 2026 for Karnataka?
faq.answer: The KSP Police Constable written exam date is 2nd August 2026. Notification 1 (KK Cadre) exam is scheduled from 2:30 PM to 4:00 PM, and Notification 2 (General Cadre) from 10:30 AM to 12:00 PM on the same day.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for KSP Constable Recruitment 2026 Karnataka?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Class 12 / PUC or equivalent from a recognised board. The age limit is 18 to 33 years for general category candidates, with relaxations for SC/ST, OBC, forest area residents, and ex-servicemen. Knowledge of the Kannada language is also mandatory.

faq.question: Q5. Where can I apply online for KSP Constable Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online at the official KEA portal: https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/. The application window is open from 8th June 2026 to 3rd July 2026.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for KSP Constable 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 750 for General and OBC (Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B) candidates. Candidates belonging to SC, ST, Category-1, Ex-Servicemen, and Transgender categories need to pay Rs. 500.

faq.question: Q7. How many vacancies are there in KSP Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 3,991 Civil Police Constable vacancies have been announced - 596 under the Kalyana-Karnataka cadre and 3,395 under the General (Mikkilida/Okkalida) cadre.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Karnataka Civil Police Constable 2026?
faq.answer: The KSP Constable selection process includes: (1) Written Exam (OMR-based, 100 marks), (2) Physical Standard Test (PST), (3) Physical Endurance Test (PET), (4) Medical Examination, and (5) Document Verification.

faq.question: Q9. What is the salary of a KSP Constable in 2026?
faq.answer: The Karnataka Police Constable salary is Rs. 37,500 - Rs. 76,100 per month as per the 7th Pay Commission (Pay Level 7), plus applicable government allowances.

faq.question: Q10. Can female candidates apply for KSP Constable Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, both male and female candidates can apply for the KSP Constable Recruitment 2026. There are separate vacancy allocations and physical standards for female and third-gender candidates.
